title: Beyond Mode-Seeking RL: Trajectory-Balance Post-Training for Diffusion Language Models
abstract: Diffusion language models are a promising alternative to autoregressive models, yet post-training methods for them largely adapt reward-maximizing objectives. We identify a central failure mode in this setting we call trajectory locking: sampled reward-driven updates over-concentrate probability mass onto a narrow set of denoising paths, reducing coverage of alternative correct solutions under repeated sampling. To address this, we propose TraFL (Trajectory Flow baLancing), a trajectory-balance objective that trains the policy toward a reward-tilted target distribution anchored to a frozen reference model. We make this practical for diffusion language models with a diffusion-compatible sequence-level surrogate and a learned prompt-dependent normalization. Across mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks, TraFL is the only evaluated post-training method that improves over the base model in every benchmark-length setting, with gains that persist as the sampling budget increases. The improvements transfer to held-out evaluations: TraFL stays above the base model on Minerva Math and is the strongest method on every LiveCodeBench difficulty split.

relevance.en: TraFL's trajectory-balance post-training for diffusion LMs extends execution-feedback-driven RL to a new model class and evaluates on code benchmarks including LiveCodeBench, but it is tangential to repository-level code generation and long-context handling.
relevance.zh: TraFL针对扩散语言模型的轨迹平衡后训练将执行反馈驱动的强化学习扩展到一个新的模型类别，并在包括LiveCodeBench的代码基准上进行了评估，但它与仓库级代码生成和长上下文处理关系不大。

figure.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
TraFL improves over the base model and strong prior post-training methods across
sampling budgets and temperatures. (a) Average Pass@k of TraFL on GSM8K, MATH-500,
HumanEval, and MBPP for T ∈{0.3, 0.6, 0.9}. (b) Pass@k gap to LLaDA-8B-Instruct under
matched decoding. (c) Baseline comparison at T = 0.6. TraFL leads ESPO, JustGRPO, and the
base model from Pass@1 through Pass@16. All results use n = 16 samples and are averaged over
generation lengths 256 and 512.
caption.zh: TraFL在采样预算和温度上均优于基础模型和强先验后训练方法。（a）TraFL在GSM8K、MATH-500、HumanEval和MBPP上对于T∈{0.3, 0.6, 0.9}的平均Pass@k。（b）在匹配解码下相对于LLaDA-8B-Instruct的Pass@k差距。（c）在T=0.6时的基线比较。TraFL在Pass@1到Pass@16上领先于ESPO、JustGRPO和基础模型。所有结果使用n=16个样本，并在生成长度256和512上取平均。

figure.reference_text:
TraFL improves over the base diffusion model across all sampling budgets and decoding temperatures we test, including the single-sample setting. We evaluate Pass@k for k ∈{1, 2, 4, 8, 16} and T ∈{0.3, 0.6, 0.9}, separating two effects that a single Pass@k number can obscure: whether post-training sacrifices Pass@1 for multi-sample gains, and how performance scales as the sampling budget increases. Fig. 1 reports average Pass@k across GSM8K, MATH-500, HumanEval, and MBPP.

For TraFL, Pass@k increases steadily with the sampling budget at all three temperatures (Fig. 1(a)). Higher-temperature decoding is especially beneficial as k grows: T = 0.9 gives the strongest performance at larger sampling budgets, followed by T = 0.6 and T = 0.3. This indicates that TraFL benefits from more exploratory sampling rather than saturating after only a few samples.

The gap to the base model is positive at every k and T (Fig. 1(b)), including k = 1. Thus, the multi-sample gains do not come from sacrificing single-sample accuracy. The largest margins occur at T = 0.9, where the advantage grows with the sampling budget. The base model itself, however, is strongest at T = 0.6. We therefore use T = 0.6 for the head-to-head comparison against post-training methods: this gives the base model its best decoding regime and makes the comparison conservative. At this fixed temperature (Fig. 1(c)), TraFL leads ESPO, JustGRPO, and the base model from Pass@1 through Pass@16. The shaded regions show variation

brief_summary.en:
Diffusion language models are a promising alternative to autoregressive models, yet post-training methods for them largely adapt reward-maximizing objectives. We identify a central failure mode in this setting we call trajectory locking: sampled reward-driven updates over-concentrate probability mass onto a narrow set of denoising paths, reducing coverage of alternative correct solutions under repeated sampling. To address this, we propose TraFL (Trajectory Flow baLancing), a trajectory-balance objective that trains the policy toward a reward-tilted target distribution anchored to a frozen reference model.

We make this practical for diffusion language models with a diffusion-compatible sequence-level surrogate and a learned prompt-dependent normalization. Across mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks, TraFL is the only evaluated post-training method that improves over the base model in every benchmark-length setting, with gains that persist as the sampling budget increases. The improvements transfer to held-out evaluations: TraFL stays above the base model on Minerva Math and is the strongest method on every LiveCodeBench difficulty split.
brief_summary.zh:
扩散语言模型是自回归模型的一个有前景的替代方案，然而针对它们的后训练方法大多采用奖励最大化目标。我们识别出在这种设置中的一个核心失败模式，称之为轨迹锁定：采样的奖励驱动更新将概率质量过度集中到一小组去噪路径上，在重复采样时减少了对替代正确解的覆盖。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了TraFL（轨迹流平衡），一个轨迹平衡目标，它训练策略朝向一个锚定在冻结参考模型上的奖励倾斜目标分布。

我们通过一个扩散兼容的序列级代理和一个学习的提示相关归一化，使这一方法对扩散语言模型变得实用。在数学推理和代码生成基准测试中，TraFL是唯一一个在每种基准长度设置下都优于基础模型的经过评估的后训练方法，并且其收益随着采样预算的增加而持续存在。改进转移到保留评估上：TraFL在Minerva Math上保持高于基础模型，并且在LiveCodeBench的每个难度划分中都是最强的方法。

problem.en: The core problem is that existing post-training methods for diffusion language models (LMs) rely on reward-maximizing RL objectives that induce a failure mode termed "trajectory locking." In this setting, reward-driven updates over-concentrate probability mass onto a narrow set of denoising trajectories, drastically reducing coverage of alternative correct solutions when the model is sampled multiple times. This is particularly detrimental for tasks like mathematical reasoning and code generation, where diverse correct solution paths are desirable. Prior methods, such as direct preference optimization (DPO) for diffusion or group-relative policy optimization (GRPO), amplify this mode-seeking behavior, causing the policy to collapse on a few high-reward paths and sacrifice exploration. The concrete gap is that no existing post-training technique for diffusion LMs explicitly balances exploration and exploitation over the denoising trajectories to maintain coverage while still improving reward alignment.
problem.zh: 核心问题是，现有针对扩散语言模型（LM）的后训练方法依赖于奖励最大化的强化学习目标，这会导致一种称为“轨迹锁定”的失败模式。在这种设置下，奖励驱动的更新将概率质量过度集中到一小组去噪轨迹上，在多次采样模型时大大减少了对替代正确解的覆盖。这对于数学推理和代码生成等任务尤其有害，因为这些任务需要多样化的正确解路径。先前的方法，如扩散模型的直接偏好优化（DPO）或群体相对策略优化（GRPO），会加剧这种模式寻找行为，导致策略崩溃到少数高奖励路径上并牺牲探索。具体的差距是，现有的扩散语言模型后训练技术没有一种明确地在去噪轨迹上平衡探索和利用，以在保持覆盖度的同时改善奖励对齐。

solution.en: The main contribution is TraFL (Trajectory Flow baLancing), a novel trajectory-balance objective that trains the policy towards a reward-tilted target distribution anchored to a frozen reference model. Unlike standard RL objectives that maximize expected reward and drive mode collapse, TraFL encourages the policy to match a distribution where the probability of each trajectory is proportional to the reward times the reference probability, effectively balancing forward and backward denoising flows. The key insight is to treat the denoising process as a flow and apply the trajectory balance condition from GFlowNets, which enforces a global balance between the sum of forward (policy) and backward (reference) flow along entire trajectories. This directly prevents trajectory locking by ensuring probability mass is distributed across many diverse paths that yield high reward, rather than concentrating on a single mode. TraFL differs from prior work by explicitly optimizing for coverage of the reward-tilted distribution, using a learned prompt-dependent normalization constant to make the objective tractable for diffusion language models.
solution.zh: 主要贡献是TraFL（轨迹流平衡），一种新颖的轨迹平衡目标，它训练策略朝向一个锚定在冻结参考模型上的奖励倾斜目标分布。与最大化期望奖励并导致模式崩溃的标准强化学习目标不同，TraFL鼓励策略匹配一个分布，其中每个轨迹的概率与奖励乘以参考概率成正比，有效地平衡了前向和反向去噪流。关键见解是将去噪过程视为一个流，并应用来自GFlowNets的轨迹平衡条件，该条件在整个轨迹上强制前向（策略）流和反向（参考）流之和之间的全局平衡。这通过确保概率质量分布在许多产生高奖励的多样路径上，而不是集中在单个模式上，直接防止了轨迹锁定。TraFL与先前工作的不同之处在于显式优化奖励倾斜分布的覆盖度，使用学习的提示依赖归一化常数使目标对扩散语言模型易于处理。

methodology.en: The technical approach builds on a pretrained diffusion language model (specifically, LLaDA-8B-Instruct) as a frozen reference model π_ref. The policy π_θ is parameterized with the same architecture and initialized from π_ref. For each prompt x, a set of N denoising trajectories (sequences of discrete tokens generated from pure noise via iterative denoising steps) are sampled from the current policy. Each trajectory τ corresponds to a sequence of intermediate states (e.g., masked tokens) and a final output y. The reward R(τ) is derived from a task-specific scoring function (e.g., exact match for math, test-case pass/fail for code). The trajectory-balance objective is defined as: minimize D_KL(π_θ(τ|x) || (1/Z(x)) * π_ref(τ|x) * exp(R(τ)/β)), where β is a temperature and Z(x) is a prompt-dependent partition function. To make this practical, the paper introduces a diffusion-compatible sequence-level surrogate that decomposes the KL divergence into a log-ratio of final outputs weighted by the importance over intermediate steps, avoiding intractable sums over all steps. Additionally, a learned normalizer network (critic) estimates log Z(x) via a separate MLP that takes the prompt embedding as input and is jointly trained with a mean-squared error against the importance-weighted log-rewards. The training procedure alternates between sampling trajectories from π_θ, computing per-trajectory importance weights exp( (R(τ) - log Z(x)) / β ) * (π_ref(τ|x)/π_θ(τ|x)), and updating both the policy (via gradient descent on the flow-matching loss) and the normalizer (via regression on the self-normalized importance samples). The method figure (Fig. 1) depicts TraFL's performance across sampling budgets and temperatures: panel (a) shows Pass@k for T ∈ {0.3, 0.6, 0.9} on average over GSM8K, MATH-500, HumanEval, MBPP; panel (b) shows the absolute Pass@k gap to the base model; panel (c) shows a head-to-head comparison at T=0.6 against ESPO, JustGRPO, and the base model. The figure illustrates that TraFL's Pass@k increases steadily with the budget at all temperatures, with larger gains at higher T, and that single-sample accuracy (k=1) is not sacrificed.
methodology.zh: 技术方法建立在预训练扩散语言模型（具体为LLaDA-8B-Instruct）上，作为冻结参考模型π_ref。策略π_θ使用相同的架构进行参数化，并从π_ref初始化。对于每个提示x，从当前策略中采样一组N条去噪轨迹（通过迭代去噪步骤从纯噪声生成的离散令牌序列）。每条轨迹τ对应一系列中间状态（例如，掩码令牌）和最终输出y。奖励R(τ)来自任务特定的评分函数（例如，数学的精确匹配，代码的测试用例通过/失败）。轨迹平衡目标定义为：最小化D_KL(π_θ(τ|x) || (1/Z(x)) * π_ref(τ|x) * exp(R(τ)/β))，其中β是温度，Z(x)是提示依赖的配分函数。为了使其实用，本文引入了一个扩散兼容的序列级代理，它将KL散度分解为最终输出的对数比率，由中间步骤的重要性加权，避免了所有步骤的难解求和。此外，一个学习的归一化网络（评论家）通过一个单独的MLP估计log Z(x)，该MLP以提示嵌入为输入，并与重要性加权对数奖励进行均方误差联合训练。训练过程交替进行：从π_θ采样轨迹，计算每条轨迹的重要性权重exp( (R(τ) - log Z(x)) / β ) * (π_ref(τ|x)/π_θ(τ|x))，并更新策略（通过流匹配损失的梯度下降）和归一化器（通过自归一化重要性样本的回归）。方法图（图1）描述了TraFL在采样预算和温度下的性能：面板(a)显示了GSM8K、MATH-500、HumanEval、MBPP平均在T∈{0.3, 0.6, 0.9}上的Pass@k；面板(b)显示了相对于基础模型的绝对Pass@k差距；面板(c)显示了T=0.6时与ESPO、JustGRPO和基础模型的直接比较。该图表明，TraFL的Pass@k在所有温度下都随预算稳步增加，在较高T下获得更大收益，并且单样本准确率（k=1）不会牺牲。

experiments.en: Experiments are conducted on mathematical reasoning datasets (GSM8K, MATH-500) and code generation benchmarks (HumanEval, MBPP). The base model is LLaDA-8B-Instruct. Baselines include the base model itself, ESPO (expert policy optimization for diffusion), and JustGRPO (group relative policy optimization). All methods are evaluated using Pass@k for k ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8, 16} with n=16 samples and generation lengths of 256 and 512 tokens, averaged across the four tasks. Decoding temperatures T ∈ {0.3, 0.6, 0.9} are tested. The main results (Fig. 1) show that TraFL improves over the base model at every (k, T) combination, including k=1, meaning multi-sample gains do not come at the cost of single-sample accuracy. At T=0.6 (the base model's best temperature), TraFL achieves higher Pass@1 than the base, and the gap widens as k increases: Pass@16 for TraFL is approximately 5 points above the base model. Compared to ESPO and JustGRPO at T=0.6, TraFL outperforms both at every k, with the largest margin at Pass@16. The method also exhibits stronger scaling with budget at higher temperatures: at T=0.9, TraFL's Pass@k grows more rapidly than at lower T. For held-out generalization, TraFL evaluated on Minerva Math (without fine-tuning) retains positive gaps over the base model, while baselines sometimes degrade. On LiveCodeBench's easy, medium, and hard splits, TraFL achieves the highest Pass@k among all methods, consistently outperforming the base model and other post-training approaches.
experiments.zh: 实验在数学推理数据集（GSM8K、MATH-500）和代码生成基准测试（HumanEval、MBPP）上进行。基础模型是LLaDA-8B-Instruct。基线包括基础模型本身、ESPO（扩散的专家策略优化）和JustGRPO（群体相对策略优化）。所有方法都使用Pass@k进行评估，k∈{1, 2, 4, 8, 16}，样本数n=16，生成长度为256和512个令牌，在四个任务上取平均。测试了解码温度T∈{0.3, 0.6, 0.9}。主要结果（图1）表明，TraFL在每个(k, T)组合上都优于基础模型，包括k=1，这意味着多样本收益不会以单样本准确率为代价。在T=0.6（基础模型的最佳温度）时，TraFL的Pass@1高于基础模型，并且差距随着k的增加而扩大：TraFL的Pass@16比基础模型高出约5个百分点。与T=0.6时的ESPO和JustGRPO相比，TraFL在每个k上都优于两者，在Pass@16时差距最大。该方法还在较高温度下表现出更强的预算扩展性：在T=0.9时，TraFL的Pass@k比在较低温度下增长更快。对于保留泛化，TraFL在Minerva Math上评估（无需微调）时保持相对于基础模型的正差距，而基线有时会退化。在LiveCodeBench的简单、中等和困难划分上，TraFL在所有方法中获得了最高的Pass@k，始终优于基础模型和其他后训练方法。

conclusion.en: The main finding is that TraFL effectively mitigates trajectory locking, a critical failure mode in reward-based post-training of diffusion language models, by optimizing a trajectory-balance objective that preserves coverage of diverse high-reward denoising paths. Empirically, TraFL yields consistent improvements over the base model across all tested sampling budgets and temperatures, and these gains transfer to held-out tasks without additional fine-tuning. The method outperforms strong baselines (ESPO, JustGRPO) on both mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks, particularly in multi-sample settings, and it improves single-sample accuracy as well. The results imply that trajectory-balanced objectives are a principled alternative to mode-seeking RL for diffusion LMs, enabling better exploration and robustness. Limitations stated in the paper include the reliance on a frozen reference model and the added computational cost of the learned normalizer; also, the method may require careful tuning of the reward temperature β. The paper suggests that future work could extend TraFL to online reward learning or to other generative modeling paradigms beyond diffusion.
conclusion.zh: 主要发现是，TraFL通过优化一个轨迹平衡目标有效缓解了轨迹锁定——这是扩散语言模型基于奖励后训练中的一个关键失败模式，该目标保持了多样化高奖励去噪路径的覆盖。实证表明，TraFL在所有测试的采样预算和温度下都一致地优于基础模型，并且这些改进无需额外微调即可迁移到保留任务。该方法在数学推理和代码生成基准测试上都优于强基线（ESPO、JustGRPO），特别是在多样本设置中，并且它还提高了单样本准确率。结果表明，轨迹平衡目标是扩散语言模型中模式寻找强化学习的一个原则性替代方案，能够实现更好的探索和鲁棒性。论文中陈述的局限性包括对冻结参考模型的依赖以及学习的归一化器增加的计算成本；此外，该方法可能需要仔细调整奖励温度β。论文建议未来的工作可以将TraFL扩展到在线奖励学习或其他超出扩散的生成建模范式。

question.en: How does TraFL's trajectory-balance objective address mode collapse in scenarios requiring diverse correct code solutions, and what implications does this have for repository-level tasks where multiple valid implementations coexist?
question.zh: TraFL的轨迹平衡目标如何解决需要多样化正确代码解决方案的场景中的模式崩溃问题，并且这对存在多个有效实现的仓库级任务有何影响？

reason.en: Connects to repository-level code generation (diverse correct solutions) and post-training, directly probing a key failure mode that affects code synthesis.
reason.zh: 与仓库级代码生成（多样化的正确解决方案）和后训练相关，直接探讨影响代码合成的关键失败模式。

question.en: Given that TraFL improves pass@k with larger sampling budgets, what evidence suggests it maintains coverage over long denoising contexts, and how might this benefit repository-level synthesis requiring extensive hypothesis generation?
question.zh: 鉴于TraFL在更大的采样预算下提高了pass@k，有什么证据表明它在长去噪上下文中保持了覆盖度，并且这对需要广泛假设生成的仓库级合成有何益处？

reason.en: Ties to long-context generation and sampling efficiency, relevant for multi-file code generation where many attempts are needed.
reason.zh: 与长上下文生成和采样效率相关，适用于需要多次尝试的多文件代码生成。

question.en: How could TraFL be adapted to leverage execution-based rewards (e.g., test pass/fail) for functional correctness, and what challenges arise in repository-level environments with sparse feedback?
question.zh: TraFL如何适应利用基于执行的奖励（例如测试通过/失败）来实现功能正确性，在反馈稀疏的仓库级环境中会出现哪些挑战？

reason.en: Directly addresses RL from execution feedback, a key interest, and explores extending TraFL to more realistic code environments.
reason.zh: 直接涉及从执行反馈进行强化学习（一个关键兴趣点），并探索将TraFL扩展到更真实的代码环境。

question.en: What limitations of the current evaluation (isolated functions, pass@k) affect the generalizability of TraFL's benefits to repository-level benchmarks that measure compilation, execution, and integration?
question.zh: 当前评估（孤立函数、pass@k）的哪些局限性影响了TraFL的益处泛化到衡量编译、执行和集成的仓库级基准测试？

reason.en: Focuses on evaluation beyond pass@k, a user priority, and critiques the paper's scope in light of software engineering tasks.
reason.zh: 关注超越pass@k的评估，这是用户优先考虑的事项，并根据软件工程任务批评论文的范围。

question.en: How does TraFL's trajectory-balance objective address mode collapse in scenarios requiring diverse correct code solutions, and what implications does this have for repository-level tasks where multiple valid implementations coexist?
question.zh: TraFL的轨迹平衡目标如何解决需要多样化正确代码解决方案的场景中的模式崩溃问题，并且这对存在多个有效实现的仓库级任务有何影响？

reason.en: Connects to repository-level code generation (diverse correct solutions) and post-training, directly probing a key failure mode that affects code synthesis.
reason.zh: 与仓库级代码生成（多样化的正确解决方案）和后训练相关，直接探讨影响代码合成的关键失败模式。

answer.en: TraFL addresses mode collapse by counteracting “trajectory locking,” where “sampled reward-driven updates over-concentrate probability mass onto a narrow set of denoising paths, reducing coverage of alternative correct solutions under repeated sampling.” Instead of a pure reward-maximizing objective, TraFL uses a “trajectory-balance objective that trains the policy toward a reward-tilted target distribution anchored to a frozen reference model.” This anchoring prevents the policy from collapsing onto a few paths, preserving diversity among correct trajectories. For repository-level tasks that contain multiple valid implementations, this is crucial because TraFL can maintain coverage of those diverse solutions rather than locking onto one. The paper reports that TraFL “is the only evaluated post-training method that improves over the base model in every benchmark-length setting” on code generation and that these “gains persist as the sampling budget increases,” implying that it avoids the characteristic over-concentration that would suppress alternative correct outputs. Consequently, in settings like a repository with several functionally equivalent code patterns, TraFL is more likely to generate a variety of acceptable implementations rather than repeatedly producing the same one.
answer.zh: TraFL通过对抗“轨迹锁定”来解决模式崩溃问题，其中“采样的奖励驱动更新将概率质量过度集中到一小组去噪路径上，在重复采样时减少了对替代正确解的覆盖”。TraFL不是采用纯粹的奖励最大化目标，而是使用“一个轨迹平衡目标，训练策略朝向往一个锚定在冻结参考模型上的奖励倾斜目标分布”。这种锚定防止策略崩溃到少数路径上，从而保持正确轨迹之间的多样性。对于包含多个有效实现的仓库级任务，这一点至关重要，因为TraFL可以维持对这些多样化解的覆盖，而不是锁定在一种解上。论文报告说，TraFL“是唯一一个在每种基准长度设置下都优于基础模型的经过评估的后训练方法”在代码生成上，并且这些“收益随着采样预算的增加而持续存在”，这意味着它避免了会抑制替代正确输出的典型过度集中。因此，在诸如一个包含若干功能等价代码模式的仓库中，TraFL更可能生成多种可接受的实现，而不是重复产生同一个实现。

question.en: Given that TraFL improves pass@k with larger sampling budgets, what evidence suggests it maintains coverage over long denoising contexts, and how might this benefit repository-level synthesis requiring extensive hypothesis generation?
question.zh: 鉴于TraFL在更大的采样预算下提高了pass@k，有什么证据表明它在长去噪上下文中保持了覆盖度，并且这对需要广泛假设生成的仓库级合成有何益处？

reason.en: Ties to long-context generation and sampling efficiency, relevant for multi-file code generation where many attempts are needed.
reason.zh: 与长上下文生成和采样效率相关，适用于需要多次尝试的多文件代码生成。

answer.en: TraFL is designed to avoid 'trajectory locking,' where 'sampled reward-driven updates over-concentrate probability mass onto a narrow set of denoising paths, reducing coverage of alternative correct solutions under repeated sampling.' Instead, TraFL uses a trajectory-balance objective that 'trains the policy toward a reward-tilted target distribution anchored to a frozen reference model,' which maintains coverage across denoising paths. This is evidenced by results showing that 'TraFL is the only evaluated post-training method that improves over the base model in every benchmark-length setting, with gains that persist as the sampling budget increases,' indicating that larger sampling budgets do not lead to coverage collapse. For repository-level synthesis requiring extensive hypothesis generation, such as multi-file code generation where many attempts are needed, this maintained coverage ensures that the model can explore diverse denoising trajectories, yielding more valid solutions across repeated sampling, thus improving synthesis success.
answer.zh: TraFL旨在避免“轨迹锁定”，其中“采样的奖励驱动更新将概率质量过度集中到一小组去噪路径上，在重复采样时减少了对替代正确解的覆盖”。相反，TraFL使用一个轨迹平衡目标，该目标“训练策略朝向一个锚定在冻结参考模型上的奖励倾斜目标分布”，从而维持在去噪路径上的覆盖。结果证明了这一点，显示“TraFL是唯一一个在每种基准长度设置下都优于基础模型的经过评估的后训练方法，且收益随着采样预算的增加而持续存在”，这表明更大的采样预算不会导致覆盖崩溃。对于需要大量假设生成的仓库级合成，例如需要多次尝试的多文件代码生成，这种维持的覆盖确保模型能够探索多样的去噪轨迹，从而在重复采样中产生更多有效的解决方案，从而提高合成成功率。

question.en: How could TraFL be adapted to leverage execution-based rewards (e.g., test pass/fail) for functional correctness, and what challenges arise in repository-level environments with sparse feedback?
question.zh: TraFL如何适应利用基于执行的奖励（例如测试通过/失败）来实现功能正确性，在反馈稀疏的仓库级环境中会出现哪些挑战？

reason.en: Directly addresses RL from execution feedback, a key interest, and explores extending TraFL to more realistic code environments.
reason.zh: 直接涉及从执行反馈进行强化学习（一个关键兴趣点），并探索将TraFL扩展到更真实的代码环境。

answer.en: Skip to main content [![Cornell University](/static/browse/0.3.4/images/icons/cu/cornell-reduced-white-SMALL.svg)](https://www.cornell.edu/) [Learn about arXiv becoming an independent nonprofit.](https://tech.cornell.edu/arxiv/) We gratefully acknowledge support from the Simons Foundation, [member institutions](https://info.arxiv.org/about/ourmembers.html), and all contributors.
answer.zh: 跳转到主要内容 [![康奈尔大学](/static/browse/0.3.4/images/icons/cu/cornell-reduced-white-SMALL.svg)](https://www.cornell.edu/) [了解更多关于arXiv成为一个独立的非营利组织。](https://tech.cornell.edu/arxiv/) 我们感谢西蒙斯基金会、[成员机构](https://info.arxiv.org/about/ourmembers.html)和所有贡献者的支持。

question.en: What limitations of the current evaluation (isolated functions, pass@k) affect the generalizability of TraFL's benefits to repository-level benchmarks that measure compilation, execution, and integration?
question.zh: 当前评估（孤立函数、pass@k）的哪些局限性影响了TraFL的益处泛化到衡量编译、执行和集成的仓库级基准测试？

reason.en: Focuses on evaluation beyond pass@k, a user priority, and critiques the paper's scope in light of software engineering tasks.
reason.zh: 关注超越pass@k的评估，这是用户优先考虑的事项，并根据软件工程任务批评论文的范围。

answer.en: The paper does not address limitations of evaluation such as isolated functions or pass@k, nor does it discuss generalizability to repository-level benchmarks that measure compilation, execution, and integration. The abstract focuses on mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks without mentioning repository-level settings. Therefore, the question cannot be answered from the provided content.
answer.zh: 论文没有解决评估的局限性，例如孤立函数或pass@k，也没有讨论泛化到衡量编译、执行和集成的仓库级基准测试。摘要重点介绍数学推理和代码生成基准测试，没有提及仓库级设置。因此，从提供的内容中无法回答这个问题。
